bench: ts-engine performance gate (docs/backlog-ast-tree-sitter.md risk #3; spec: docs/specs/perf-lazy-ast.md)
question: does the tree-sitter tier cost stay acceptable as the default?
method: cold extraction, best of 3 subprocess runs per arm, --no-ctags; the regex arm opts out explicitly (--engine regex) — the previous no-flag arm silently benchmarked tree-sitter against itself once the tier went default-on in v9

axios.bench: ts-engine (cold extraction, regex vs tree-sitter tier)

self.bench: ts-engine (cold extraction, regex vs tree-sitter tier)

warmPath.note: with Spec A (lazy init + AST products in the scan cache), a warm cached refresh never initializes the engine
warmPath.measured: codeweb self, 172 files: warm 0.38s (ast: idle, 0 rescans) vs 1.89s before Spec A; cold 2.13s unchanged; fragments byte-identical

decision.rationale: cold builds pay ~+1.35 ms/symbol ONCE per changed file for exact complexity + dispatch recall (the oracle A/B recall-1.00 input); every cached run — the MCP auto-refresh, the post-edit hook, staleness checks — is engine-free after Spec A. The cost sits where users wait least; --engine regex remains the opt-out.
